target_statement: 分析 Riemann zeta 函数在临界线上零点的连续间距统计性质，特别是小间距（相对于平均间距）的分布和理论下界。在 RH 假设下，探索是否存在 μ < 1 使得 lim inf (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) / (2π/log γ_n) < μ，以及该常数的最优值。

propositions.statement: Riemann Hypothesis (RH): all non-trivial zeros of ζ(s) lie on the critical line Re(s) = 1/2.
The zero counting function N(T) := #{ρ : 0 < Im(ρ) ≤ T} satisfies N(T) ~ (T/2π) log(T/2π) - T/2π.
Let ρ_n = 1/2 + iγ_n be the n-th zero ordered by increasing imaginary part. Define the normalized gap d_n := (γ_{n+1} - γ_n)·log(γ_n)/(2π); the average normalized gap equals 1.
Under RH, Goldston proved ∫₀ᵀ S'(t)^2 dt ~ T(log T)^2/(12π^2), where S(t) is the argument function for ζ(1/2+it).
Under RH, lim inf d_n < 0.50895 is proven (cited as a current best result in the route).
Relation linking normalized gaps and S-increments: d_n + ΔS_n = 1 + O(1/log γ_n), where ΔS_n = S(γ_{n+1}) - S(γ_n), derived from N(γ_{n+1}) - N(γ_n) = 1 and the decomposition N(t)=N0(t)+S(t).
Define consecutive small-gap pair count N₂(μ,T) := #{n : γ_n ∈ [T,2T], d_n < μ, d_{n+1} < μ}.
If d_n < μ and d_{n+1} < μ, then ΔS_n > 1-μ - O(1/log γ_n) and ΔS_{n+1} > 1-μ - O(1/log γ_n).
If d_n < μ and d_{n+1} < μ with γ_n ∈ [T,2T], then γ_{n+2} - γ_n < 4πμ/log T.
On (γ_n,γ_{n+2}) with d_n,d_{n+1}<μ, S increases by ≥ 2(1-μ) - O(1/log T); by Cauchy–Schwarz, ∫_{γ_n}^{γ_{n+2}} S'(t)^2 dt ≥ (1-μ)^2 log T/(πμ) (up to lower-order errors).
From all indices counted by N₂(μ,T), one can extract at least N₂(μ,T)/3 disjoint intervals (γ_n,γ_{n+2}) (a combinatorial disjointness extraction).
Summing over the disjoint subcollection gives (N₂/3)·(1-μ)^2 log T/(πμ) ≤ ∫_T^{2T} S'(t)^2 dt (up to lower-order errors).
Combining with Goldston’s second-moment formula yields N₂(μ,T) ≤ μ·T log T/(4π(1-μ)^2)·(1+o(1)).
With M := N(2T)-N(T) ~ T log T/(2π), the normalized bound is N₂(μ,T)/M ≤ μ/(2(1-μ)^2)·(1+o(1)).
Solving μ/(2(1-μ)^2)=1 gives threshold μ=1/2 (the route notes a possible earlier mistaken golden-ratio claim).
Sample evaluations: at μ=1/3, bound gives N₂/M ≤ 3/8; at μ=0.4, bound gives N₂/M ≤ 0.4/(2·0.36) ≈ 0.556.
GUE predicts the probability of two consecutive gaps both < μ equals ∬_{[0,μ]^2} p₂(s₁,s₂) ds₁ds₂; for μ=0.4 it is ~0.02 (as stated in the route).
At μ=0.4, the derived bound ~0.556 is much weaker than the GUE prediction ~0.02 but is consistent (upper bound).
Testable prediction for Odlyzko data near height 10^20: fraction of consecutive pairs with d_n,d_{n+1}<0.4 should be ≤ 0.556 and empirically near ~0.02 if GUE holds.
A Goldston–Montgomery extremal alternating distribution can have N₂=0, so the consecutive-pair constraint alone does not directly improve the single-gap liminf constant (e.g., 0.50895).
Montgomery pair correlation for |α|<1 forbids perfect alternation, so combining pair-correlation constraints with the consecutive-pair constraint may yield new information (speculative).
Claimed risk item: propagating the O(1/log γ_n) error in d_n+ΔS_n leads only to an additional O(T^{1/2+ε}) term in the final bound (requires justification).
Main derived theorem (route): under RH, N₂(μ,T) ≤ (μ/(2(1-μ)^2))·(N(2T)-N(T)) + O(T^{1/2+ε}); non-trivial for μ<1/2, proved using Goldston’s ∫S'^2 second moment and Cauchy–Schwarz.
Target statement (restated): analyze consecutive gap statistics for zeta zeros, ask whether some μ<1 has lim inf d_n < μ, and seek the optimal constant.

chain.statement: Use N(T) asymptotics to define normalized gaps and note mean gap 1.
From N(γ_{n+1})-N(γ_n)=1 and N(t)=N0(t)+S(t), derive d_n+ΔS_n=1+O(1/log γ_n).
Introduce N₂(μ,T) counting indices with two successive normalized gaps below μ.
Translate d_n<μ and d_{n+1}<μ into lower bounds on ΔS_n and ΔS_{n+1} via the gap–S relation.
Bound γ_{n+2}-γ_n using the two small-gap conditions and normalized gap definition.
Apply Cauchy–Schwarz on (γ_n,γ_{n+2}) using S increment and interval length to obtain a lower bound for ∫ S'(t)^2.
Extract a disjoint subcollection of at least N₂/3 intervals (γ_n,γ_{n+2}).
Sum the per-interval ∫S'^2 lower bounds over disjoint intervals to bound ∫_T^{2T} S'(t)^2 from below.
Compare the lower bound with Goldston’s asymptotic for ∫S'^2 and solve for N₂(μ,T).
Normalize by M=N(2T)-N(T) to obtain N₂/M ≤ μ/(2(1-μ)^2).
Determine when μ/(2(1-μ)^2) < 1 by solving μ/(2(1-μ)^2)=1.
Compute sample numerical bounds at μ=1/3 and μ=0.4.
Compare the derived upper bound at μ=0.4 with the GUE predicted probability.
Translate the bound and GUE prediction into a check against Odlyzko data.
Explain why the consecutive-pair constraint does not directly improve the best known liminf gap constant via an extremal alternating distribution example.
Assemble the preceding bounds into a theorem statement including an error term caveat.
Address the target using: existing liminf bound (0.50895), the new consecutive-gap constraint, and GUE/Odlyzko comparisons for the conjectural optimum.

summary: 在 RH 下，通过引入连续小间距对计数 N₂(μ,T) 并结合 Goldston 的 ∫S'^2 二阶矩与 Cauchy–Schwarz，得到连续小间距聚集的上界 N₂/M ≤ μ/(2(1-μ)^2)（非平凡阈值 μ<1/2）。该约束不直接改进已知 lim inf d_n < 0.50895，但提供了可与 GUE/数值数据对比的新型聚集限制。
